content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `origin/master...HEAD` excluding `.ralph-burning`. I found three fix-now issues in the new propose-new-bead flow: replay is not idempotent, dependency placement is still missing, and the created-bead ID fallback can resolve the wrong duplicate.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Propose-new-bead reconciliation is not replay-safe. `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success` replays every accepted `ProposeNewBead` from the latest aggregate on every success-reconciliation rerun, but `handle_propose_new_bead` has no journal-based dedupe and reclassifies an already-created same-title bead as planned-elsewhere. After a post-create crash or any normal replay, the same finding can flip from `ProposedBeadCreated` into a spurious planned-elsewhere mapping instead of recognizing the prior creation.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:979-1027
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2876-2901
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2957-2983
remediation_hint: Make propose-new-bead handling idempotent by checking the milestone journal for an existing creation record keyed by run/completion_round/finding before replaying, or teach the handler to return the previously created bead instead of recording a planned-elsewhere mapping when the created bead already exists.
   Rationale: `reconcile_success` explicitly treats post-close reconciliation as replayable, so changing durable outcome on rerun is a correctness bug, not just missing polish.
2. finding_summary: The new handler never injects any dependency edge for the bead it creates. It creates the bead, syncs, and journals it, but there is no call to `BrMutationAdapter::add_dependency` or any other graph-placement logic, so the follow-up work is left orphaned from the dependency graph even though this bead’s scope requires dependency placement and the journal is supposed to record where the bead was placed.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2921-2983
remediation_hint: After bead creation, add the appropriate dependency edge(s) before `sync_flush()`, and include the chosen placement in the `ProposedBeadCreated` journal metadata and tests.
   Rationale: Without graph placement, `br ready`/`bv` can surface the new bead with the wrong readiness semantics, and the implementation does not satisfy the bead’s dependency-injection requirement.
3. finding_summary: `resolve_created_bead_id` falls back to a raw title lookup that ignores the duplicate filters used by the defensive re-check. If `br create` stdout ever stops yielding a parseable ID, the fallback can return a closed duplicate or the active bead itself, even though those cases are explicitly rejected during pre-check, and the wrong bead ID will then be written to the journal as the created follow-up.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2756-2795
remediation_hint: Reuse `eligible_existing_proposed_bead_match` (or a stricter predicate) in the fallback path, and prefer a structured create response or another unambiguous lookup over first-title-match resolution.
   Rationale: The implementation already acknowledges that closed/self title matches are invalid during duplicate detection; reintroducing those invalid matches in the create-ID fallback makes the creation path brittle and can corrupt audit records.
